// ==== KoineRuntime.php ====
<?php
// <auto-generated/>
// Koine PHP runtime: shared domain primitives, emitted once.
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Koine\Runtime;

use \InvalidArgumentException;
use \RuntimeException;

/**
 * Thrown when a domain invariant or illegal state transition is violated.
 */
class DomainInvariantViolationException extends RuntimeException
{
    public function __construct(
        public readonly string $typeName,
        public readonly string $rule,
    ) {
        parent::__construct("Invariant violated on {$typeName}: {$rule}");
    }
}

/**
 * Thrown when an optimistic-concurrency version check fails on a versioned aggregate.
 */
class ConcurrencyConflictException extends RuntimeException
{
    public function __construct(
        public readonly string $typeName,
        public readonly int $expected,
        public readonly int $actual,
    ) {
        parent::__construct(
            "Concurrency conflict on {$typeName}: expected version {$expected}, was {$actual}"
        );
    }
}

/**
 * A closed interval [start, end] over an orderable value, with containment and overlap.
 *
 * The comparator is optional: when absent the default PHP comparison operators (`<`, `<=`)
 * are used, which work for int, float, and string. Supply a callable(mixed,mixed):int
 * (negative / zero / positive) for custom ordering, e.g. on value objects.
 *
 * @template T
 */
class Range
{
    /** @param T $start
     * @param T $end
     * @param (callable(T,T):int)|null $comparator
     */
    public function __construct(
        public readonly mixed $start,
        public readonly mixed $end,
        private readonly mixed $comparator = null,
    ) {
        if ($this->cmp($start, $end) > 0) {
            throw new DomainInvariantViolationException(
                'Range',
                'start must be less than or equal to end',
            );
        }
    }

    /** @param T $a @param T $b */
    private function cmp(mixed $a, mixed $b): int
    {
        if ($this->comparator !== null) {
            return ($this->comparator)($a, $b);
        }
        return $a <=> $b;
    }

    /** Returns true when $value lies within [start, end] inclusive.
     * @param T $value
     */
    public function contains(mixed $value): bool
    {
        return $this->cmp($value, $this->start) >= 0
            && $this->cmp($value, $this->end) <= 0;
    }

    /** Returns true when this range shares at least one point with $other.
     * @param Range<T> $other
     */
    public function overlaps(self $other): bool
    {
        return $this->cmp($this->start, $other->end) <= 0
            && $this->cmp($other->start, $this->end) <= 0;
    }
}

/**
 * A value that folds with itself under add() — the seam the generic Decimal::sum() helper
 * binds to (@template T of Summable) so a value-object projection sums to its own type and
 * stays phpstan --level max clean (issue #692). The native parameter is the interface type so
 * an implementing value object never trips PHP's parameter-contravariance rule; the @param self
 * narrows it back for the implementer body, and @return static preserves the element type.
 */
interface Summable
{
    /**
     * @param self $other
     * @return static
     */
    public function add(self $other): self;
}

/**
 * A value with a total order via compareTo() — the seam the generic Decimal::min()/max()
 * helpers bind to (@template T of Comparable). Value objects are not orderable in the model
 * (KOI0212 rejects a value-object min/max selector), so Decimal is the only implementer today;
 * the bound keeps the Decimal-element min/max type-preserving and symmetric with Summable.
 */
interface Comparable
{
    /** @param self $other */
    public function compareTo(self $other): int;
}

/**
 * A string-backed arbitrary-precision decimal value (no float).
 *
 * Arithmetic delegates to PHP's BCMath extension (bcadd, bcsub, bcmul, bcdiv).
 * The scale (number of decimal places) defaults to 10; supply a higher value for
 * use-cases that demand finer precision.
 */
final class Decimal implements Summable, Comparable
{
    /** @var numeric-string The validated decimal string; numeric by construction. */
    private string $value;

    public function __construct(string|int $value, private readonly int $scale = 10)
    {
        $normalized = (string)$value;
        if (!is_numeric($normalized)) {
            throw new InvalidArgumentException("Invalid Decimal value: {$value}");
        }
        // is_numeric() above narrows $normalized to numeric-string, which the bc-math
        // helpers (bcadd/bcsub/bcmul/bcdiv/bccomp) require under phpstan --level max.
        $this->value = $normalized;
    }

    public function getValue(): string
    {
        return $this->value;
    }

    /** @param self $other */
    public function add(Summable $other): self
    {
        return new self(bcadd($this->value, $other->value, $this->scale), $this->scale);
    }

    public function sub(self $other): self
    {
        return new self(bcsub($this->value, $other->value, $this->scale), $this->scale);
    }

    public function mul(self $other): self
    {
        return new self(bcmul($this->value, $other->value, $this->scale), $this->scale);
    }

    public function div(self $other): self
    {
        if (bccomp($other->value, '0', $this->scale) === 0) {
            throw new DomainInvariantViolationException('Decimal', 'division by zero');
        }
        return new self(bcdiv($this->value, $other->value, $this->scale), $this->scale);
    }

    /** @param self $other */
    public function compareTo(Comparable $other): int
    {
        return bccomp($this->value, $other->value, $this->scale);
    }

    public function equals(self $other): bool
    {
        return $this->compareTo($other) === 0;
    }

    public function __toString(): string
    {
        return $this->value;
    }

    /**
     * Sums a non-empty array of decimals, folding under add() from the first element.
     *
     * A string-backed decimal has no implicit zero, so an EMPTY array raises a domain error
     * rather than fabricating one (parity with the C#/TypeScript/Python empty-fold guard).
     * A value-object projection whose add() returns its own type folds here too, and the
     * @template T of Summable bound keeps the result the element's own type (issue #692):
     * an array<Money> sums to a Money, not a Decimal, under phpstan --level max.
     *
     * @template T of Summable
     * @param array<T> $values
     * @return T
     */
    public static function sum(array $values): Summable
    {
        if ($values === []) {
            throw new DomainInvariantViolationException('collection', 'cannot sum an empty collection (no zero value)');
        }
        $items = array_values($values);
        $total = $items[0];
        $count = count($items);
        for ($i = 1; $i < $count; $i++) {
            $total = $total->add($items[$i]);
        }
        return $total;
    }

    /**
     * The minimum element of a non-empty array, ordered via compareTo().
     *
     * An EMPTY array raises a domain error (a collection with no element has no minimum),
     * mirroring the C#/TypeScript/Python empty-fold guard.
     *
     * @template T of Comparable
     * @param array<T> $values
     * @return T
     */
    public static function min(array $values): Comparable
    {
        if ($values === []) {
            throw new DomainInvariantViolationException('collection', 'cannot take min of an empty collection (no value)');
        }
        $items = array_values($values);
        $result = $items[0];
        $count = count($items);
        for ($i = 1; $i < $count; $i++) {
            if ($items[$i]->compareTo($result) < 0) {
                $result = $items[$i];
            }
        }
        return $result;
    }

    /**
     * The maximum element of a non-empty array, ordered via compareTo().
     *
     * An EMPTY array raises a domain error (a collection with no element has no maximum),
     * mirroring the C#/TypeScript/Python empty-fold guard.
     *
     * @template T of Comparable
     * @param array<T> $values
     * @return T
     */
    public static function max(array $values): Comparable
    {
        if ($values === []) {
            throw new DomainInvariantViolationException('collection', 'cannot take max of an empty collection (no value)');
        }
        $items = array_values($values);
        $result = $items[0];
        $count = count($items);
        for ($i = 1; $i < $count; $i++) {
            if ($items[$i]->compareTo($result) > 0) {
                $result = $items[$i];
            }
        }
        return $result;
    }
}

/**
 * Generic query-handler contract: handles a typed query and returns a typed result.
 *
 * Implement this interface for each query handler in the application layer.
 *
 * @template TQuery
 * @template TResult
 */
interface QueryHandler
{
    /** @param TQuery $query @return TResult */
    public function handle(mixed $query): mixed;
}

// ==== composer.json ====
{
    "name": "koine/generated",
    "description": "Generated by Koine — do not edit by hand.",
    "type": "library",
    "require": {
        "php": ">=8.1"
    },
    "autoload": {
        "psr-4": {
            "Koine\\": "src/"
        }
    },
    "config": {
        "sort-packages": true
    }
}

// ==== phpstan.neon ====
# <auto-generated/>
parameters:
    level: max
    paths:
        - src

// ==== src/Shop/ValueObjects/Basket.php ====
<?php
// <auto-generated/>
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Koine\Shop\ValueObjects;

/**
 * A basket whose total folds its money lines whole — the element AND result are Money,
 * so the fold must preserve the value-object type (the #692 path), not collapse to Decimal.
 */
final class Basket
{
    /** @param list<Money> $lines */
    public function __construct(
        public readonly array $lines
    ) {
    }

    public function total(): Money
    {
        return \Koine\Runtime\Decimal::sum(array_map(fn($l) => $l, $this->lines));
    }

    public function equals(self $other): bool
    {
        return $this->lines === $other->lines;
    }
}

// ==== src/Shop/ValueObjects/Money.php ====
<?php
// <auto-generated/>
declare(strict_types=1);

namespace Koine\Shop\ValueObjects;

/** A monetary amount. Never negative. */
final class Money implements \Koine\Runtime\Summable
{
    public function __construct(
        public readonly \Koine\Runtime\Decimal $amount
    ) {
        if ($amount->compareTo(new \Koine\Runtime\Decimal('0')) < 0) {
            throw new \Koine\Runtime\DomainInvariantViolationException("Money", 'an amount cannot be negative');
        }
    }

    public function equals(self $other): bool
    {
        return $this->amount->equals($other->amount);
    }

    /** @param self $other */
    public function add(\Koine\Runtime\Summable $other): self
    {
        return new self(
            $this->amount->add($other->amount)
        );
    }
}
